nodes.label: AI as sensitive first-line screening tool
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable or missing critical items
nodes.grounding_span: we definitely try to adjust the settings of the AI platform so that it is highly sensitive and can flag more accounting entries than needed so that nothing is missed

nodes.label: Human judgment as final decision-maker
nodes.grounding_span: allowing us to make the final decision

nodes.label: AI for routine standardized communication
nodes.label_negative: AI for nuanced client relationship emails
nodes.grounding_span: I was able to utilize AI to somewhat automate this process, including having it draft emails in this situation (which I feel more comfortable with as they follow a standard template)

nodes.label: AI as efficiency booster freeing up judgment work
nodes.label_negative: AI as job replacer threatening roles
nodes.grounding_span: I view it more as a way to reduce the amount of hours individuals focus on data entry and marking up client support. This would allow us to increase the number of audits we do in a year, as well as allow us more to focus on judgment calls

nodes.label: Personalized client communication
nodes.label_negative: Robotic or accusatory tone from AI
nodes.grounding_span: I do still prefer to write the message myself instead of having AI do it. This is often because I've built a friendly yet professional connection with the client, and want to make sure the email does not come across as accusatory or 'robotic'

nodes.label: Client relationship trust and professionalism
nodes.grounding_span: I've built a friendly yet professional connection with the client

nodes.label: Professional efficiency and productivity
nodes.grounding_span: reduce the amount of hours individuals focus on data entry
